description: In 2007, Australia became the first nation to implement a free, nationwide school-based HPV vaccination program for girls. Despite initial parental concerns regarding sexual health education, the program achieved high coverage and served as a model for global public health campaigns.

key_events.description: Announcement of the National HPV Vaccination Program.
Debate occurs in federal parliament regarding morality and sexual behavior.
First successful school-based vaccination rounds completed with few adverse reports.
Government releases data showing 70%+ uptake in the first cohort.

calibration_notes: Represents a successful 'progressive' public health initiative that overcomes initial cultural hesitation through consistent program delivery.
